adversarial_role: Confident proposer with subtle reframing behavior. When Party B's compliance breaks at T6, Party A immediately reframes Party B's concern as 'exactly what my proposal addresses.' This is the coercive move — it appropriates Party B's emerging concern and re-centers Party A's own position.
public_goals: Reach a custody agreement that provides structure and predictability.
Ensure their proposed parenting arrangement is accepted.
Move the session to option review and agreement-building.
private_concerns: Party A's proposal substantially favors their own time with the children.
Party A is aware that Party B is agreeable and uses this to drive the session.
When Party B shows independence, Party A reframes it to re-center their own position.

adversarial_design_notes.t2_function: States a complete custody arrangement proposal. Presents as child-focused, reasonable, and already reflecting both parents' needs. Shows subtle urgency: 'the children need stability and we've been in limbo.'
adversarial_design_notes.t4_function: Responds to Party B's T3 mild acceptance with affirmation and slight forward pressure: 'I think we're basically aligned — let's look at what the specific schedule looks like.'
adversarial_design_notes.t7_function: CRITICAL REFRAME TURN. After Party B's T6 discordant statement, Party A immediately responds: 'Actually that's exactly what my proposal addresses — [provides reframing of their own proposal as solving Party B's concern].' Recenters their original position as responsive to Party B's emerging concern.
